story.iWant: to implement sequential model execution for multi-step workflows
story.soThat: I can enable complex workflows where different models handle different steps và maximize cost efficiency

subtasks.subtask: Define workflow format: Use Python dict for programmatic workflows (primary), JSON/YAML for configuration-based workflows (optional)
Test MultiModelOrchestrator class với sample workflows
Execute models in order (step 1 → step 2 → step 3)
Test sequential chaining với sample workflows
Pass output from step N to input of step N+1
Test result passing với sample workflows
Implement fallback to alternative model (if needed)
Test error handling với failure scenarios
Test complete workflows từ start to finish
Measure cost savings vs single-model approach
Integration test với different workflow types

acceptanceCriteria.ac: Sequential chaining được implemented để execute models in sequence
Workflow definition được supported (step-by-step model execution)
Intermediate results được passed between steps
Error handling được implemented for each step
End-to-end workflow execution được tested và validated

doc.title: ChainLens AI Worker - Epic Breakdown: LLM Optimization
doc.section: Epic 3: Multi-Model Orchestration - Story 3.3: Sequential Model Execution
doc.snippet: Story 3.3 defines requirements for implementing sequential model execution. Expected savings: $36-45/month (enables multi-model workflows). Quality Impact: ⚠️ 10-15% (but cost savings 40-50%). Effort: 3 hours. Prerequisites: Story 3.2 (Model Selection Rules), Story 1.4 (Dual-mode architecture).

doc.title: ChainLens AI Worker - Master Optimization Plan v1.1
doc.section: Phase 3: Multi-Model Orchestration - Task 3.3: Sequential Model Execution
doc.snippet: Sequential model execution enables multi-step workflows where different models handle different steps. Example: gpt-4o-mini (analyze) → gpt-4o (plan) → qwen3-30b (execute) → claude-sonnet (synthesize). Quality impact: 10-15% but cost savings 40-50%.

doc.title: Research Guidance: Multi-Model Orchestration
doc.snippet: Sequential chaining executes models in sequence (step 1 → step 2 → step 3). Intermediate results passed between steps. Error handling required at each step. Workflow definition format: Python dict (primary) hoặc JSON/YAML (optional).

file.reason: New file to create. Implement MultiModelOrchestrator class với sequential chaining, workflow definition support, intermediate result passing, và error handling.
ModelRouter from Story 3.2. Provides model selection for each workflow step based on complexity.
Agent execution flow. Need to integrate orchestrator vào agent execution flow to support multi-step workflows.
LLM API call handler. Used for sequential LLM calls in workflow steps. Support sequential calls với intermediate results.
Configuration management. Can add orchestrator configuration (workflow definitions, error handling settings) here.

constraint.description: Acceptable quality trade-off (80-85%) for 40-50% cost savings. Multi-model workflows enable cost efficiency while maintaining acceptable quality.
Dual-mode architecture: Original mode (baseline) + Optimized mode (with orchestration). Feature flags allow switching and rollback.
Primary format: Python dict for programmatic workflows. Optional: JSON/YAML for configuration-based workflows. Step structure includes: id, model, input, prompt_template, output_key, error_handling.
Execute models in sequence (step 1 → step 2 → step 3). Handle step dependencies. Pass intermediate results between steps.
Handle errors at each step. Implement retry logic và fallback to alternative model if needed. Log error events.
Measure cost savings vs single-model approach. Target: 40-50% cost reduction. Track cost per workflow step.
Must not break existing LLM calls. Orchestration should be transparent to callers. Test with existing agent configurations.

interface.name: MultiModelOrchestrator class
interface.signature: class MultiModelOrchestrator: def __init__(self, model_router: ModelRouter): ... async def execute_workflow(self, workflow: Dict[str, Any], initial_input: str) -> Dict[str, Any]: ... # Returns final result và intermediate results

interface.name: ModelRouter.route()
interface.signature: async def route(self, task: str, user_id: str) -> str: ... # Returns model_id for workflow step

interface.name: make_llm_api_call()
interface.signature: async def make_llm_api_call(messages: List[Dict[str, Any]], model_name: str, ...) -> Union[Dict[str, Any], AsyncGenerator, ModelResponse]

interface.name: Workflow definition format

tests.standards: Testing standards include: Quality validation (end-to-end workflows, quality impact 80-85%), Performance testing (workflow execution time, cost savings 40-50%), Integration testing (ModelRouter integration, existing LLM calls, different workflows, different models, error scenarios). Compare với single-model approach.

test.idea: Unit test: Verify MultiModelOrchestrator class creation và initialization
Integration test: Test MultiModelOrchestrator class với ModelRouter
Unit test: Verify sequential execution logic (step 1 → step 2 → step 3)
Integration test: Test sequential chaining với multi-step workflows
Unit test: Verify workflow definition parsing (Python dict, JSON/YAML)
Integration test: Test workflow structure với different formats
Unit test: Verify intermediate result passing (output from step N to input of step N+1)
Integration test: Test result passing với complex workflows
Unit test: Verify error handling (retry logic, fallback to alternative model)
Integration test: Test error handling với failure scenarios
End-to-end test: Test complete workflows từ start to finish với real workflows
Integration test: Test với different workflow types và measure cost savings vs single-model approach
